PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE SOUGHT LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGING BREACH OF THE PURPORTED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BEFORE FILING THIS MOTION. MATERIALITY ON A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS DETERMINED BY THE PLEADINGS. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT MS. ISVORANU'S MARCH 16, 2016 EMAIL IS AN ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT, MS. ISVORANU'S DECLARATION AND THE E-MAIL ITSELF SHOW THAT THE CONTRACT IS AMBIGUOUS AND THUS EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE IS ADMISSIBLE TO DETERMINE ITS MEANING. MS. ISVORANU'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THAT EMAIL WAS INTENDED TO BE INCLUSIVE OR EXCLUSIVE OF FEES PRECLUDING THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE THIS MOTION WAS FILED IN GOOD FAITH AND IS NOT FRIVOLOUS. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. Off Calendar Jul-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Bradley J. Mancuso of Bohm Law Group (916-927-5574) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Delia Isvoranu of Sedgwick LLP (415-627-1578) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The matter is argued and submitted by both sides.

PLAINTIFF DIANE YAMAMOTO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied, but she is granted twenty days leave to file an amended complaint alleging a claim for breach of contract based on the purported settlement. Plaintiff should have sought leave to file an amended complaint alleging breach of the purported settlement agreement before filing this motion. Materiality on a summary judgment motion is determined by the pleadings. Assuming without deciding that Ms. Isvoranu's March 16, 2016 email is an enforceable contract, Ms. Isvoranu's declaration and the e-mail itself show that the contract is ambiguous and thus extrinsic evidence is admissible to determine its meaning. Ms. Isvoranu's declaration creates a triable issue whether that email was intended to be inclusive or exclusive of fees precluding the granting of this motion. Defendants' request for sanctions is denied because this motion was filed in good faith and is not frivolous. Counsel for defendant shall prepare a written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, Bus. Tel: 415-717-9768. (D302)
